title: The Wind Was Rough Which Tore
content:
The wind was rough which tore
    That leaf from its parent tree
    The fate was cruel which bore
    The withering corpse to me

    We wander on we have no rest
    It is a dreary way

    What shadow is it
    That ever moves before [my] eyes
    It has a brow of ghostly whiteness
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Bronte, titled "The Wind Was Rough Which Tore", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
